ops.text: PRE-REGISTRATION E32 (Wave 0). Hypothesis: a frontier model (sonnet) given K in-context steering exemplars as a byte-constant cached prefix can act as the watcher and match/beat the FT qwen3-8b watcher (incumbent 0.9317 AUC) on the frozen n=628 frame — a prompt-only alternative to fine-tuning. Data: n=628 watcher frame (335 pos/293 neg, digest e85dc4a2); exemplars drawn from the watcher TRAIN split ONLY (never the 628 — session-atomic, no exemplar shares a session with any eval row). Infra: cc_steer/retrain/manyshot.py (shipped db4c6914) — build_exemplar_system(byte-constant, seed-det) + score_frame batching 20 rows/call against the shared prefix, writing probs via evalset.write_probs (paired-comparable), asserting cache_read>0 and aborting on a cache-miss streak. Arms: K ∈ {125, 250, 500, 1000} exemplars. FUNNEL: each K screened on a fixed 200-row subset of the 628 first; finalists (K that look competitive) scored on the full 628. KILL RULE: if AUC is non-increasing 125→500 on the screen, stop (more shots aren't helping) — don't run 1000. PRIMARY: paired DeLong (cc_steer.instrument) vs the incumbent 0.9317 on the full 628 — actionable per the instrument-card rule (CI excludes 0 AND |Δ|≥frame MDE). CO-PRIMARY (reported, not gating): $/1k-turns and latency vs the served MLX watcher — a frontier watcher that ties on AUC but costs 100x/is slower is an ops story for the user, not an auto-adopt. Cache economics (E32 probe): batched 15-25/call against a byte-constant prefix, 1-hour cache amortizes the exemplar prefix (~$13-22 full-corpus vs $109-275 per-row). $25 HARD CAP; abort at $22 projected; running ledger. Harness ~/.cc-steer/experiments/E32/ own venv, cc-steer @ git+file@main, sonnet via spawnllm (eval "$(ccp env)"). VERIFY: independent DeLong recompute from persisted probs vs the incumbent's stored fire_base; exemplar-vs-eval session-disjointness audit; cache_read>0 per batch; ledger ≤ cap. No artifact ships — a win is a promotion question to the user (frontier watcher = ceiling-not-necessarily-servable, cf E37).
